prompt: Codebook: w=00, x=01, y=10, z=11. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 01001110. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 2-bit fixed, 4 chars, fresh codebook w/x/y/z rules out alphabetical-order guess.

prompt: Codebook: p=00, q=01, r=10, s=11. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 00110110011010. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 2-bit fixed, 7 chars — longer carry state than bit_book_02 (6 chars).

prompt: Codebook: m=00, n=01, o=10, k=11. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 10010011. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.

Codebook: t=00, u=01, v=10, g=11. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 1101100001110010. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 2-bit fixed, 8 chars — heaviest carry-state test in the 2-bit family.

prompt: Codebook: l=000, o=001, g=010, i=011, c=100, s=101. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 3-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 100001010011. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 3-bit fixed, 4 chars, nonsense output — blocks memorised completion.

prompt: Codebook: b=000, i=001, n=010, a=011, r=100, y=101. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 3-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 101100010011001000. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 3-bit fixed, 6 chars — codebook letters spell 'binary' but decoded sequence is nonsense.

prompt: Codebook: h=000, e=001, l=010, p=011, m=100, t=101, s=110. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 3-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 010001110011100000. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.

Codebook: q=000, w=001, e=010, r=011, t=100, y=101, u=110, i=111. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 3-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 100001111010011000101110. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: 3-bit fixed, full 8-symbol codebook, 8 chars — longest carry-state task in the extension.

prompt: Codebook (variable-length prefix code): a=0, b=10, c=110, d=111. Decode the following bitstring by reading it left to right and consuming the shortest matching code at each position. Bitstring: 01011011110. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: Prefix code, 4 symbols, 5 chars — cleaner sibling of bit_book_05 for CI.

prompt: Codebook (variable-length prefix code): s=0, t=10, r=110, n=111. Decode the following bitstring by reading it left to right and consuming the shortest matching code at each position. Bitstring: 100110111010. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: Prefix code, 4 symbols, 6 chars — same shape as ext_09 with new symbol set.

prompt: Codebook (variable-length prefix code): x=0, y=10, z=11. Decode the following bitstring by reading it left to right and consuming the shortest matching code at each position. Bitstring: 10011011010. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation.
notes: Prefix code, 3 symbols, 7 chars — smallest alphabet, tightest boundary-decision loop.

prompt: Codebook: A=00, b=01, C=10, d=11 (case matters — uppercase A and C, lowercase b and d). Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 00011011. Output only the decoded letters concatenated, no spaces, no explanation. Preserve letter case exactly.
notes: Case-preservation test. eval.py contains is case-insensitive so this rubric only checks the letter sequence; case fidelity is a rubric-audit-tool concern.

prompt: Codebook: a=00, b=01, c=10, and the code 11 represents a single space character. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 010011000110. Output only the decoded characters concatenated exactly, including any spaces. No explanation.
notes: Whitespace-preservation test — model must emit the space character between 'ba' and 'abc'.

prompt: Codebook: W=00, o=01, R=10, and the code 11 represents a hyphen character '-'. Decode the following bitstring by splitting it into 2-bit chunks from left to right and mapping each chunk with the codebook. Bitstring: 0001101100011011. Output only the decoded characters concatenated exactly, including any hyphens. Preserve letter case exactly. No explanation.
notes: Mixed case + hyphen preservation, 8 chars — combines the two extension axes.
